name: Herbie Nichols
abstract: Herbert Horatio Nichols (January 3, 1919 – April 12, 1963) was an American jazz pianist and composer who wrote the jazz standard "Lady Sings the Blues". Obscure during his lifetime, he is now highly regarded by many musicians and critics.

about_quotes.speaker: Roswell Rudd
about_quotes.evidence: Herbie Nichols, the pianist who was my friend for about the last three years of his life.
about_quotes.doc_title: Roswell Rudd interviewed by Monk Rowe, Ellenville, New York, June 23, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Sheila Jordan
about_quotes.evidence: Herbie Nichols took me on a musical, spiritual trip... he wrote the changes that he wanted on it... the way he played underneath me
about_quotes.doc_title: Sheila Jordan — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Wilbur Ware
about_quotes.evidence: the following weekend, Herbie Nichols -- I was able to play with him
about_quotes.doc_title: 68088_Wilbur B. Ware, Sr oral history interview

about_quotes.speaker: Marian McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: I never did. I didn’t know much about his music. I just knew him, because he died quite a few years ago, didn’t he?
about_quotes.doc_title: Marian McPartland — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
